title: Speed / Distance / Time
subject: math
summary: This topic focuses on motion, travel time, relative speed, and average speed.
SPTG usually tests practical word problems rather than difficult theory.
Common traps include average speed, same direction vs opposite direction, and unit conversion.
coreConcepts: Distance = speed × time is the main foundation of the whole topic.
Average speed must be total distance divided by total time, not always the average of two speeds.
Relative speed is added when objects move toward each other and subtracted when they move in the same direction.
Units must be consistent, such as km/h with hours or m/s with seconds.
Round-trip questions often trap students into using the wrong average-speed method.
derive: Speed means distance traveled per unit time, so speed = distance / time.
Rearranging again gives time = distance / speed.
Average speed is defined as total distance divided by total time.
For relative speed, the change in distance between objects determines whether to add or subtract speeds.
formulas: average speed = total distance / total time
relative speed (toward each other) = v1 + v2

sptgSet.question: A car travels at 60 km/h for 2 hours. How far does it travel?
A person walks 10 km in 2 hours. Find the average speed.
A bus travels 150 km at 50 km/h. How long does it take?
Two trains move toward each other at 40 km/h and 60 km/h. If they are 300 km apart, how long before they meet?
Two runners move in the same direction at 12 km/h and 8 km/h. If the faster runner starts 20 km behind, how long to catch up?
A car goes 100 km at 50 km/h and another 100 km at 100 km/h. Find the average speed for the whole trip.
A train travels at 72 km/h. Convert this speed to m/s.
A man drives from city A to B at 60 km/h and returns at 40 km/h. Find the average speed for the whole journey.
A boat travels 30 km downstream in 2 hours and returns upstream in 3 hours. Find the speed of the boat in still water.
Two cyclists start from the same point in opposite directions at 15 km/h and 25 km/h. How far apart will they be after 3 hours?

hardSet.question: A car covers the first half of a journey at 40 km/h and the second half at 60 km/h. Find the average speed for the whole trip.
A train travels 120 km at 60 km/h and then 180 km at 90 km/h. Find its average speed for the entire trip.
Two cars are 420 km apart and drive toward each other at 50 km/h and 70 km/h. After how many hours will they be 60 km apart?
Two runners move around a circular track in the same direction at 10 m/s and 6 m/s. If the track length is 400 m, how long until the faster runner laps the slower runner once?
A man walks to a town at 5 km/h and returns along the same route at 3 km/h. Find his average speed for the round trip.
A bus leaves at 8:00 AM at 40 km/h. Another bus leaves the same point at 10:00 AM at 60 km/h in the same direction. At what time will the second bus catch the first?
A river current flows at 3 km/h. A boat moves at 15 km/h in still water. Find the downstream and upstream speeds.
A cyclist travels for 2 hours at 20 km/h, then for 1.5 hours at 16 km/h. Find the total distance and average speed.
A car travels 240 km. If it increases speed by 20 km/h, the trip takes 1 hour less. Find the original speed.
A person travels from A to B partly by walking and partly by bus. He walks 6 km at 4 km/h, then takes a bus for 30 km at 36 km/h. Find the average speed for the entire trip.
